service.description:
Backend job: publish all READY social posts across all tenants whose scheduledDate is in the past (or unset). Designed to run as a Moqui ServiceJob — no user context required. Credentials are looked up per post using the post's own ownerPartyId. Errors per post are recorded but do not abort the batch.
Approval gate: a post adapted from a MasterContent (post.masterContentId set) only publishes once that MasterContent.approvedDate is set — a human approves the master piece once and every platform variant auto-publishes from then on. Posts with no masterContentId (created directly, not via adapt#ContentForPlatform) are treated as already approved.
Daily limit: at most PlatformConfiguration.dailyLimit posts are published per ownerPartyId+platform per UTC day, counting posts already PUBLISHED today. SUBSTACK_NOTE is counted in its own bucket but shares the SUBSTACK configuration's dailyLimit (it shares the SUBSTACK credentials too).

service-call.name: update#growerp.marketing.SocialPost
service-call.in-map: [postId: post.postId, status: 'PUBLISHED', publishedDate: ec.user.nowTimestamp, publishedUrl: publishResult.publishedUrl, externalPostId: publishResult.externalPostId, publishError: null, lastModifiedDate: ec.user.nowTimestamp]

service-call.name: update#growerp.marketing.SocialPost
service-call.in-map: [postId: post.postId, publishError: publishResult.errorMessage ?: 'Unknown error', lastModifiedDate: ec.user.nowTimestamp]

service.description: Publish a social post to its configured platform. Routes to platform-specific service based on post.platform. On success updates status to PUBLISHED and sets publishedDate/publishedUrl. On failure sets publishError and leaves status unchanged.

return.message: No platform set on social post ${postId}
No PlatformConfiguration found for platform ${post.platform}. Please configure credentials first.
No API publisher for ${post.platform}; use the assisted content send-queue for this platform

service-call.name: update#growerp.marketing.SocialPost
service-call.in-map: [ postId: postId, status: 'PUBLISHED', publishedDate: ec.user.nowTimestamp, publishedUrl: publishResult.publishedUrl, externalPostId: publishResult.externalPostId, publishError: null, lastModifiedDate: ec.user.nowTimestamp ]

service-call.name: update#growerp.marketing.SocialPost
service-call.in-map: [ postId: postId, publishError: publishResult.errorMessage, lastModifiedDate: ec.user.nowTimestamp ]

service.description: Create a draft post on Substack via private API, then publish it (send: true also emails all subscribers). If the publish step fails the draft remains recoverable on Substack and the error includes the draft URL.
Publish a post to LinkedIn via UGC Posts API v2
Publish a post to a Facebook Page via the Graph API
Publish an article to Medium via the Medium API
Publish a post to X (Twitter) via API v2, signed with OAuth 1.0a user-context (HMAC-SHA1). The JSON body is not part of the OAuth signature — only the URL and OAuth parameters are signed.
